text: 2.16 Preparation and characterization of Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que To prepare Ab-Fc-EL-EV, the isolated Fc-EL-EVs were co-incubated with either the Fc-engineered anti-Prdx1 antibody or control IgG-GFP. Leveraging the specific high-affinity binding of the Z-domain on the surface of Fc-EL-EVs to the Fc fragment, the antibodies were stably conjugated onto the surface of the EVs. After sufficient incubation and washing to remove unbound free antibodies, functionalized Fc-EL-EVs with conjugated antibodies, designated as Ab-Fc-EL-EV, were obtained. Finally, to achieve drug loading, the prepared Ab-Fc-EL-EVs were co-incubated with Quercetin. By optimizing the incubation conditions, Quercetin was efficiently encapsulated into the internal space of the Ab-Fc-EL-EVs. After centrifugation and washing to remove free Quercetin, the engineered EVs loaded with Quercetin, designated as Ab-Fc-EL-EV@Que, were finally obtained.
